Children.Caption: Sales This Month
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the sum of sales in the current month excluding taxes.
Children.CustomTooltipText: Specifies the sum of sales in the current month excluding taxes.

Children.Caption: Overdue Sales Invoice Amount
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the sum of overdue payments from customers.
Children.CustomTooltipText: Specifies the sum of overdue payments from customers.

Children.Caption: Overdue Purch. Invoice Amount
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the sum of your overdue payments to vendors.
Children.CustomTooltipText: Specifies the sum of your overdue payments to vendors.

Children.Caption: Sales Invoices Predicted Overdue
Children.Synopsis: Specifies sales invoices that are predicted to be paid late.
Children.CustomTooltipText: Specifies sales invoices that are predicted to be paid late.

Children.Caption: Return to Getting Started
Children.Synopsis: Learn how to get started with Dynamics 365.

Children.Caption: Sales Quotes
Children.Synopsis: Specifies sales quotes that have not yet been converted to invoices or orders.
Children.CustomTooltipText: Specifies sales quotes that have not yet been converted to invoices or orders.

Children.Caption: Sales Orders
Children.Synopsis: Specifies sales orders that are not yet posted or only partially posted.
Children.CustomTooltipText: Specifies sales orders that are not yet posted or only partially posted.

Children.Caption: Completely Reserved from Stock
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the number of sales orders that are completely reserved from stock.
Children.CustomTooltipText: Specifies the number of sales orders that are completely reserved from stock.

Children.Caption: Sales Invoices
Children.Synopsis: Specifies sales invoices that are not yet posted or only partially posted.
Children.CustomTooltipText: Specifies sales invoices that are not yet posted or only partially posted.

Children.Caption: Sales Invoices - Pending Document Exchange
Children.Synopsis: Specifies sales invoices that await sending to the customer through the document exchange service.
Children.CustomTooltipText: Specifies sales invoices that await sending to the customer through the document exchange service.

Children.Caption: Sales Credit Memos - Pending Document Exchange
Children.Synopsis: Specifies sales credit memos that await sending to the customer through the document exchange service.
Children.CustomTooltipText: Specifies sales credit memos that await sending to the customer through the document exchange service.

Children.Caption: Purchase Orders
Children.Synopsis: Specifies purchases orders that are not posted or only partially posted.
Children.CustomTooltipText: Specifies purchases orders that are not posted or only partially posted.

Children.Caption: Ongoing Purchase Invoices
Children.Synopsis: Specifies purchases invoices that are not posted or only partially posted.
Children.CustomTooltipText: Specifies purchases invoices that are not posted or only partially posted.

Children.Caption: Purch. Invoices Due Next Week
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the number of payments to vendors that are due next week.
Children.CustomTooltipText: Specifies the number of payments to vendors that are due next week.

Children.Caption: Pending Inbox Transactions
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the number of pending incoming intercompany transactions.
Children.CustomTooltipText: Specifies the number of pending incoming intercompany transactions.

Children.Caption: Pending Outbox Transactions
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the number of pending outgoing intercompany transactions.
Children.CustomTooltipText: Specifies the number of pending outgoing intercompany transactions.

Children.Caption: Unprocessed Payments
Children.Synopsis: Specifies imported bank transactions for payments that are not yet reconciled in the Payment Reconciliation Journal window.
Children.CustomTooltipText: Specifies imported bank transactions for payments that are not yet reconciled in the Payment Reconciliation Journal window.

Children.Caption: Average Collection Days
Children.Synopsis: Specifies how long customers took to pay invoices in the last three months. This is the average number of days from when invoices are issued to when customers pay the invoices.
Children.CustomTooltipText: Specifies how long customers took to pay invoices in the last three months. This is the average number of days from when invoices are issued to when customers pay the invoices.

Children.Caption: Outstanding Vendor Invoices
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the number of invoices from your vendors that have not been paid yet.
Children.CustomTooltipText: Specifies the number of invoices from your vendors that have not been paid yet.

Children.Caption: Create Incoming Doc. from Camera
Children.Synopsis: Create an incoming document by taking a photo of the document with your device camera. The photo will be attached to the new document.

Children.Caption: My Incoming Documents
Children.Synopsis: Specifies incoming documents that are assigned to you.
Children.CustomTooltipText: Specifies incoming documents that are assigned to you.

Children.Caption: Inc. Doc. Awaiting Verification
Children.Synopsis: Specifies incoming documents in OCR processing that require you to log on to the OCR service website to manually verify the OCR values before the documents can be received.
Children.CustomTooltipText: Specifies incoming documents in OCR processing that require you to log on to the OCR service website to manually verify the OCR values before the documents can be received.

Children.Caption: Integration Errors
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the number of errors related to data integration.
Children.CustomTooltipText: Specifies the number of errors related to data integration.

Children.Caption: Coupled Data Synchronization Errors
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the number of errors that occurred in the latest synchronization of coupled data between Business Central and Dynamics 365 Sales.
Children.CustomTooltipText: Specifies the number of errors that occurred in the latest synchronization of coupled data between Business Central and Dynamics 365 Sales.

Children.Caption: Try with my own data
Children.Synopsis: Set up My Company with the settings you choose. We'll show you how, it's easy.

Children.Caption: Replay Getting Started
Children.Synopsis: Show the Getting Started guide again.

controlTree.Caption: License Configuration
controlTree.AboutText: License configuration allows you to set up the default permissions that new users will get when accessing the system.

Children.Caption: Home
Children.ToolTip: Manage your business. See KPIs, trial balance, and favorite customers.

Children.Caption: Customers
Children.ToolTip: View or edit detailed information for the customers that you trade with. From each customer card, you can open related information, such as sales statistics and ongoing orders, and you can define special prices and line discounts that you grant if certain conditions are met.

Children.Caption: Vendors
Children.ToolTip: View or edit detailed information for the vendors that you trade with. From each vendor card, you can open related information, such as purchase statistics and ongoing orders, and you can define special prices and line discounts that the vendor grants you if certain conditions are met.

Children.Caption: Items
Children.ToolTip: View or edit detailed information for the products that you trade in. The item card can be of type Inventory or Service to specify if the item is a physical unit or a labor time unit. Here you also define if items in inventory or on incoming orders are automatically reserved for outbound documents and whether order tracking links are created between demand and supply to reflect planning actions.

Children.Caption: Bank Accounts
Children.ToolTip: View or set up detailed information about your bank account, such as which currency to use, the format of bank files that you import and export as electronic payments, and the numbering of checks.

Children.Caption: Chart of Accounts
Children.ToolTip: View or organize the general ledger accounts that store your financial data. All values from business transactions or internal adjustments end up in designated G/L accounts. Business Central includes a standard chart of accounts that is ready to support businesses in your country, but you can change the default accounts and add new ones.

Children.Caption: Finance
Children.ToolTip: Post financial transactions, manage budgets, analyze G/L  data, and prepare financial statements.

Children.Caption: General Journals
Children.ToolTip: Post financial transactions directly to general ledger accounts and other accounts, such as bank, customer, vendor, and employee accounts. Posting with a general journal always creates entries on general ledger accounts. This is true even when, for example, you post a journal line to a customer account, because an entry is posted to a general ledger receivables account through a posting group.

Children.Caption: Chart of Accounts
Children.ToolTip: View or organize the general ledger accounts that store your financial data. All values from business transactions or internal adjustments end up in designated G/L accounts. Business Central includes a standard chart of accounts that is ready to support businesses in your country, but you can change the default accounts and add new ones.

Children.Caption: G/L Account Categories
Children.ToolTip: Personalize the structure of your financial statements by mapping general ledger accounts to account categories. You can create category groups by indenting subcategories under them. Each grouping shows a total balance. When you choose the Generate Financial Reports action, the row definitions for the underlying financial reports are updated. The next time you run one of these reports, such as the balance statement, new totals and subentries are added, based on your changes.

Children.Caption: G/L Budgets
Children.ToolTip: View summary information about the amount budgeted for each general ledger account in different time periods.

Children.Caption: Fixed Assets
Children.ToolTip: Manage periodic depreciation of your machinery or machines, keep track of your maintenance costs, manage insurance policies related to fixed assets, and monitor fixed asset statistics.

Children.Caption: Financial Reporting
Children.ToolTip: Get insight into the financial data stored in your chart of accounts. Financial reports analyze figures in G/L accounts, and compare general ledger entries with general ledger budget entries. For example, you can view the general ledger entries as percentages of the budget entries. Financial reports provide the data for core financial statements and views, such as the Cash Flow chart.

Children.Caption: Sales Budgets
Children.ToolTip: Enter item sales values of type amount, quantity, or cost for expected item sales in different time periods. You can create sales budgets by items, customers, customer groups, or other dimensions in your business. The resulting sales budgets can be reviewed here or they can be used in comparisons with actual sales data in sales analysis reports.

Children.Caption: Purchase Budgets
Children.ToolTip: Enter item purchases values of type amount, quantity, or cost for expected item purchases in different time periods. You can create purchase budgets by items, vendors, vendor groups, or other dimensions in your business. The resulting purchase budgets can be reviewed here or they can be used in comparisons with actual purchases data in purchase analysis reports.

Children.Caption: Sales Analysis Reports
Children.ToolTip: Analyze the dynamics of your sales according to key sales performance indicators that you select, for example, sales turnover in both amounts and quantities, contribution margin, or progress of actual sales against the budget. You can also use the report to analyze your average sales prices and evaluate the sales performance of your sales force.

Children.Caption: Purchase Analysis Reports
Children.ToolTip: Analyze the dynamics of your purchase volumes. You can also use the report to analyze your vendors' performance and purchase prices.

Children.Caption: Inventory Analysis Reports
Children.ToolTip: Analyze the dynamics of your inventory according to key performance indicators that you select, for example inventory turnover. You can also use the report to analyze your inventory costs, in terms of direct and indirect costs, as well as the value and quantities of your different types of inventory.

Children.Caption: VAT Returns
Children.ToolTip: Prepare the VAT Return report so you can submit VAT amounts to a tax authority.

Children.Caption: Currencies
Children.ToolTip: View the different currencies that you trade in or update the exchange rates by getting the latest rates from an external service provider.

Children.Caption: Employees
Children.ToolTip: View or modify employees' details and related information, such as qualifications and pictures, or register and analyze employee absence. Keeping up-to-date records about your employees simplifies personnel tasks. For example, if an employee's address changes, you register this on the employee card.

Children.Caption: VAT Statements
Children.ToolTip: View a statement of posted VAT amounts, calculate your VAT settlement amount for a certain period, such as a quarter, and prepare to send the settlement to the tax authorities.

Children.Caption: Dimensions
Children.ToolTip: View or edit dimensions, such as area, project, or department, that you can assign to sales and purchase documents to distribute costs and analyze transaction history.

Children.Caption: Statistical Accounts
Children.ToolTip: Define statistical accounts for tracking non-transactional data.

Children.Caption: Allocation Accounts
Children.ToolTip: Define Allocation accounts that can be used to distribute amounts on general journal lines or allocation account lines in documents to different accounts based on fixed or variable percentages.

Children.Caption: Posted General Journals
Children.ToolTip: Open the list of posted general journal lines.

Children.Caption: Intrastat Reports
Children.ToolTip: Summarize the value of your purchases and sales with business partners in the EU for statistical purposes and prepare to send it to the relevant authority.

Children.Caption: Cash Management
Children.ToolTip: Process incoming and outgoing payments. Set up bank accounts and service connections for electronic banking.

Children.Caption: Cash Flow Forecasts
Children.ToolTip: Combine various financial data sources to find out when a cash surplus or deficit might happen or whether you should pay down debt, or borrow to meet upcoming expenses.

Children.Caption: Chart of Cash Flow Accounts
Children.ToolTip: View a chart contain a graphical representation of one or more cash flow accounts and one or more cash flow setups for the included general ledger, purchase, sales, services, or fixed assets accounts.
Children.Source: [{ "page": "Business Manager Role Center"}, { "action": "Chart of Cash Flow Accounts"}]

Children.Caption: Cash Flow Manual Revenues
Children.ToolTip: Record manual revenues, such as rental income, interest from financial assets, or new private capital to be used in cash flow forecasting.
Children.Source: [{ "page": "Business Manager Role Center"}, { "action": "Cash Flow Manual Revenues"}]

Children.Caption: Cash Flow Manual Expenses
Children.ToolTip: Record manual expenses, such as salaries, interest on credit, or planned investments to be used in cash flow forecasting.
Children.Source: [{ "page": "Business Manager Role Center"}, { "action": "Cash Flow Manual Expenses"}]

Children.Caption: Cash Receipt Journals
Children.ToolTip: Register received payments by manually applying them to the related customer, vendor, or bank ledger entries. Then, post the payments to G/L accounts and thereby close the related ledger entries.

Children.Caption: Payment Journals
Children.ToolTip: Register payments to vendors. A payment journal is a type of general journal that is used to post outgoing payment transactions to G/L, bank, customer, vendor, employee, and fixed assets accounts. The Suggest Vendor Payments functions automatically fills the journal with payments that are due. When payments are posted, you can export the payments to a bank file for upload to your bank if your system is set up for electronic banking. You can also issue computer checks from the payment journal.

Children.Caption: Bank Accounts
Children.ToolTip: View or set up detailed information about your bank account, such as which currency to use, the format of bank files that you import and export as electronic payments, and the numbering of checks.

Children.Caption: Bank Acc. Statements
Children.ToolTip: View statements for selected bank accounts. For each bank transaction, the report shows a description, an applied amount, a statement amount, and other information.

Children.Caption: Payment Recon. Journals
Children.ToolTip: Reconcile unpaid documents automatically with their related bank transactions by importing a bank statement feed or file. In the payment reconciliation journal, incoming or outgoing payments on your bank are automatically, or semi-automatically, applied to their related open customer or vendor ledger entries. Any open bank account ledger entries related to the applied customer or vendor ledger entries will be closed when you choose the Post Payments and Reconcile Bank Account action. This means that the bank account is automatically reconciled for payments that you post with the journal.

Children.Caption: Direct Debit Collections
Children.ToolTip: Instruct your bank to withdraw payment amounts from your customer's bank account and transfer them to your company's account. A direct debit collection holds information about the customer's bank account, the affected sales invoices, and the customer's agreement, the so-called direct-debit mandate. From the resulting direct-debit collection entry, you can then export an XML file that you send or upload to your bank for processing.

Children.Caption: Payment Terms
Children.ToolTip: Set up the payment terms that you select from on customer cards to define when the customer must pay, such as within 14 days.

Children.Caption: Bank Deposits
Children.ToolTip: Manage bank deposits to your bank accounts.

Children.Caption: Bank Account Reconciliations
Children.ToolTip: Reconcile bank accounts in your system with bank statements received from your bank.

Children.Caption: Sales
Children.ToolTip: Make quotes, orders, and credit memos to customers. Manage customers and view transaction history.

Children.Caption: Customers
Children.ToolTip: View or edit detailed information for the customers that you trade with. From each customer card, you can open related information, such as sales statistics and ongoing orders, and you can define special prices and line discounts that you grant if certain conditions are met.

Children.Caption: Items
Children.ToolTip: View or edit detailed information for the products that you trade in. The item card can be of type Inventory or Service to specify if the item is a physical unit or a labor time unit.

Children.Caption: Item Charges
Children.ToolTip: View or edit the codes for item charges that you can assign to purchase and sales transactions to include any added costs, such as freight, physical handling, and insurance that you incur when purchasing or selling items. This is important to ensure correct inventory valuation. For purchases, the landed cost of a purchased item consists of the vendor's purchase price and all additional direct item charges that can be assigned to individual receipts or return shipments. For sales, knowing the cost of shipping sold items can be as vital to your company as knowing the landed cost of purchased items.

Children.Caption: Sales Quotes
Children.ToolTip: Make offers to customers to sell certain products on certain delivery and payment terms. While you negotiate with a customer, you can change and resend the sales quote as much as needed. When the customer accepts the offer, you convert the sales quote to a sales invoice or a sales order in which you process the sale.

Children.Caption: Sales Orders
Children.ToolTip: Record your agreements with customers to sell certain products on certain delivery and payment terms. Sales orders, unlike sales invoices, allow you to ship partially, deliver directly from your vendor to your customer, initiate warehouse handling, and print various customer-facing documents. Sales invoicing is integrated in the sales order process.

Children.Caption: Blanket Sales Orders
Children.ToolTip: Use blanket sales orders as a framework for a long-term agreement between you and your customers to sell large quantities that are to be delivered in several smaller shipments over a certain period of time. Blanket orders often cover only one item with predetermined delivery dates. The main reason for using a blanket order rather than a sales order is that quantities entered on a blanket order do not affect item availability and thus can be used as a worksheet for monitoring, forecasting, and planning purposes..

Children.Caption: Sales Invoices
Children.ToolTip: Register your sales to customers and invite them to pay according to the delivery and payment terms by sending them a sales invoice document. Posting a sales invoice registers shipment and records an open receivable entry on the customer's account, which will be closed when payment is received. To manage the shipment process, use sales orders, in which sales invoicing is integrated.

Children.Caption: Sales Credit Memos
Children.ToolTip: Revert the financial transactions involved when your customers want to cancel a purchase or return incorrect or damaged items that you sent to them and received payment for. To include the correct information, you can create the sales credit memo from the related posted sales invoice or you can create a new sales credit memo with copied invoice information. If you need more control of the sales return process, such as warehouse documents for the physical handling, use sales return orders, in which sales credit memos are integrated. Note: If an erroneous sale has not been paid yet, you can simply cancel the posted sales invoice to automatically revert the financial transaction.

Children.Caption: Sales Return Orders
Children.ToolTip: Compensate your customers for incorrect or damaged items that you sent to them and received payment for. Sales return orders support warehouse documents for the item handling, the ability to return items from multiple sales documents with one return, and automatic creation of related sales credit memos or other return-related documents, such as a replacement sales order.

Children.Caption: Reminders
Children.ToolTip: Remind customers about overdue amounts based on reminder terms and the related reminder levels. Each reminder level includes rules about when the reminder will be issued in relation to the invoice due date or the date of the previous reminder and whether interests are added. Reminders are integrated with finance charge memos, which are documents informing customers of interests or other money penalties for payment delays.

Children.Caption: Finance Charge Memos
Children.ToolTip: Send finance charge memos to customers with delayed payments, typically following a reminder process. Finance charges are calculated automatically and added to the overdue amounts on the customer's account according to the specified finance charge terms and penalty/interest amounts.

Children.Caption: Posted Sales Invoices
Children.ToolTip: Open the list of posted sales invoices.

Children.Caption: Posted Sales Credit Memos
Children.ToolTip: Open the list of posted sales credit memos.
Children.Source: [{ "page": "Business Manager Role Center"}, { "action": "Posted Sales Credit Memos"}]

Children.Caption: Posted Sales Return Receipts
Children.ToolTip: Open the list of posted sales return receipts.
Children.Source: [{ "page": "Business Manager Role Center"}, { "action": "Posted Sales Return Receipts"}]

Children.Caption: Posted Sales Shipments
Children.ToolTip: Open the list of posted sales shipments.

Children.Caption: Issued Reminders
Children.ToolTip: View the list of issued reminders.

Children.Caption: Issued Finance Charge Memos
Children.ToolTip: View the list of issued finance charge memos.
Children.Source: [{ "page": "Business Manager Role Center"}, { "action": "Issued Finance Charge Memos"}]

Children.Caption: Purchasing
Children.ToolTip: Manage purchase invoices and credit memos. Maintain vendors and their history.

Children.Caption: Vendors
Children.ToolTip: View or edit detailed information for the vendors that you trade with. From each vendor card, you can open related information, such as purchase statistics and ongoing orders, and you can define special prices and line discounts that the vendor grants you if certain conditions are met.

Children.Caption: Incoming Documents
Children.ToolTip: Handle incoming documents, such as vendor invoices in PDF or as image files, that you can manually or automatically convert to document records, such as purchase invoices. The external files that represent incoming documents can be attached at any process stage, including to posted documents and to the resulting vendor, customer, and general ledger entries.

Children.Caption: Item Charges
Children.ToolTip: View or edit the codes for item charges that you can assign to purchase and sales transactions to include any added costs, such as freight, physical handling, and insurance that you incur when purchasing or selling items. This is important to ensure correct inventory valuation. For purchases, the landed cost of a purchased item consists of the vendor's purchase price and all additional direct item charges that can be assigned to individual receipts or return shipments. For sales, knowing the cost of shipping sold items can be as vital to your company as knowing the landed cost of purchased items.

Children.Caption: Purchase Quotes
Children.ToolTip: Create purchase quotes to represent your request for quotes from vendors. Quotes can be converted to purchase orders.

Children.Caption: Purchase Orders
Children.ToolTip: Create purchase orders to mirror sales documents that vendors send to you. This enables you to record the cost of purchases and to track accounts payable. Posting purchase orders dynamically updates inventory levels so that you can minimize inventory costs and provide better customer service. Purchase orders allow partial receipts, unlike with purchase invoices, and enable drop shipment directly from your vendor to your customer. Purchase orders can be created automatically from PDF or image files from your vendors by using the Incoming Documents feature.

Children.Caption: Blanket Purchase Orders
Children.ToolTip: Use blanket purchase orders as a framework for a long-term agreement between you and your vendors to buy large quantities that are to be delivered in several smaller shipments over a certain period of time. Blanket orders often cover only one item with predetermined delivery dates. The main reason for using a blanket order rather than a purchase order is that quantities entered on a blanket order do not affect item availability and thus can be used as a worksheet for monitoring, forecasting, and planning purposes.

Children.Caption: Purchase Invoices
Children.ToolTip: Create purchase invoices to mirror sales documents that vendors send to you. This enables you to record the cost of purchases and to track accounts payable. Posting purchase invoices dynamically updates inventory levels so that you can minimize inventory costs and provide better customer service. Purchase invoices can be created automatically from PDF or image files from your vendors by using the Incoming Documents feature.

Children.Caption: Purchase Credit Memos
Children.ToolTip: Create purchase credit memos to mirror sales credit memos that vendors send to you for incorrect or damaged items that you have paid for and then returned to the vendor. If you need more control of the purchase return process, such as warehouse documents for the physical handling, use purchase return orders, in which purchase credit memos are integrated. Purchase credit memos can be created automatically from PDF or image files from your vendors by using the Incoming Documents feature. Note: If you have not yet paid for an erroneous purchase, you can simply cancel the posted purchase invoice to automatically revert the financial transaction.
Children.Source: [{ "page": "Business Manager Role Center"}, { "action": "\u003CPage Purchase Credit Memos\u003E"}]

Children.Caption: Purchase Return Orders
Children.ToolTip: Create purchase return orders to mirror sales return documents that vendors send to you for incorrect or damaged items that you have paid for and then returned to the vendor. Purchase return orders enable you to ship back items from multiple purchase documents with one purchase return and support warehouse documents for the item handling. Purchase return orders can be created automatically from PDF or image files from your vendors by using the Incoming Documents feature. Note: If you have not yet paid for an erroneous purchase, you can simply cancel the posted purchase invoice to automatically revert the financial transaction.

Children.Caption: Posted Purchase Invoices
Children.ToolTip: Open the list of posted purchase invoices.
Children.Source: [{ "page": "Business Manager Role Center"}, { "action": "\u003CPage Posted Purchase Invoices\u003E"}]

Children.Caption: Posted Purchase Credit Memos
Children.ToolTip: Open the list of posted purchase credit memos.
Children.Source: [{ "page": "Business Manager Role Center"}, { "action": "\u003CPage Posted Purchase Credit Memos\u003E"}]

Children.Caption: Posted Purchase Receipts
Children.ToolTip: Open the list of posted purchase receipts.
Children.Source: [{ "page": "Business Manager Role Center"}, { "action": "\u003CPage Posted Purchase Receipts\u003E"}]

Children.Caption: Posted Purchase Return Shipments
Children.ToolTip: Open the list of posted purchase return shipments.
Children.Source: [{ "page": "Business Manager Role Center"}, { "action": "Posted Purchase Return Shipments"}]

Children.Caption: Quality Inspections
Children.ToolTip: See existing Quality Inspections and create a new inspection.

Children.Caption: Quality Inspection Lines
Children.ToolTip: Historical Quality Inspection lines. Use this with analysis mode.

Children.Caption: Shopify
Children.ToolTip: Manage Shopify Shops, customers, products, orders, gift cards, transactions and payouts.

Children.Caption: Shops
Children.ToolTip: Define your Shopify Shops and enter how to synchronize data for each shop.

Children.Caption: Customers
Children.ToolTip: View or edit detailed information for the customers that you trade with through Shopify.

Children.Caption: Products
Children.ToolTip: Add, view or edit detailed information for the products that you trade in through Shopify.

Children.Caption: Orders
Children.ToolTip: View your Shopify agreements with customers to sell certain products on certain delivery and payment terms.

Children.Caption: Refunds
Children.ToolTip: View your Shopify refunds.

Children.Caption: Returns
Children.ToolTip: View your Shopify returns.

Children.Caption: Gift Cards
Children.ToolTip: View the Shopify Gift Cards, their unique code and balance.

Children.Caption: Transactions
Children.ToolTip: View every single movement of money in or out the Shopify Payment account.

Children.Caption: Payouts
Children.ToolTip: View the movement of money between a Shopify Payment account balance and a connected bank account.

Children.Caption: Delete
Children.Synopsis: Delete the selected row

Children.Caption: Refresh
Children.Synopsis: Update the page with any changes made by other users.

Children.Caption: Open in Excel
Children.Synopsis: Opens an Excel file with the data exported from the list (selected filters and columns).

Children.Caption: Add License Configuration
Children.Synopsis: Add a configuration for a license.

Children.Caption: Configure
Children.Synopsis: Customize license permissions.

Children.Caption: Users
Children.Synopsis: Manage permissions for users that are already tracked in Business Central, and update user information from Microsoft 365.

Children.Caption: Business Central admin center
Children.Synopsis: Manage security groups and license-related features for each environment.

Children.Caption: Microsoft 365 admin center
Children.Synopsis: Assign licenses to existing users or create new users.

Children.Name: Control_View_Actions
Children.Synopsis: Switch to another view of License Configuration.
